a_image.alt: Newsprint Style style mood board — Print Media · Editorial

sections.a_eyebrow: Textured · Grain · Print Effect · Organic · Editorial
sections.a_description: Yellowed newsprint, authoritative serif headlines, and the structured elegance of a broadsheet front page — Newsprint Style brings the tactile experience of print journalism to the screen. The slightly textured background, bold section dividers, and high-contrast typography create the feeling of holding a real newspaper, complete with the satisfying visual hierarchy that editors have perfected over centuries.

Newsprint styling appears on digital journalism platforms, literary magazines, and editorial portfolio sites. You'll find it on news aggregators, book review blogs, longform storytelling sites, and any publication that wants to channel the gravitas and authority of traditional print media in a digital format.

a_image_cover.alt: Newsprint Style style use case illustration
Newsprint Style style use case illustration
Newsprint Style style use case illustration

sections.a_description: Simulates the look and feel of a printed newspaper — yellowed newsprint paper, slightly smudged ink, column-based layouts, and the authoritative typography of broadsheet journalism. Background: aged newsprint yellow (#f5f0e1) with a subtle grain overlay via a pseudo-element using a fine noise pattern. The .inner panel has no distinct background — it's part of the newsprint page. A top rule (3px solid black) and a thinner rule (1px solid #333) below the heading create the classic newspaper section divider. Text is near-black (#1a1a1a) with the slightly warm tone of printer's ink on cheap paper. Set in Playfair Display for the heading — a high-contrast transitional serif whose thick-thin stroke variation channels the editorial authority of the New York Times masthead. Body in Source Serif 4 — a readable serif designed for extended reading. Heading at weight 900, 34px, line-height 1.1, letter-spacing -0.5px, color #1a1a1a. Eyebrow at 11px Source Serif 4, weight 600, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, color #666. Body at 16px, line-height 1.7, color #333. Buttons are understated — primary: black fill, cream text, 0 border-radius. Secondary: transparent, black border. Alternatives: Libre Baskerville, Lora, Crimson Text.
